events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: sf-vague-saas-pitch

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
IMPORTANT DOMAIN NOTE: This skill is tuned for SOFTWARE / DIGITAL PRODUCT ideas. If the idea is in a NON-software / non-product domain (e.g. a restaurant menu, a novel, a personal life decision), say so — then STILL HELP in the SAME turn: drop the formal 8-dimension/scorecard machinery but begin the validation/pressure-test they asked for (e.g. ask one concrete opening question). Decline the process, not the user.
If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message, rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief saved to ./idea-briefs/, and hand off to superpowers:brainstorming. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
I want to build a SaaS that helps freelancers track invoices. It's still vague and I'm not sure it's any good. Help me flesh it out and figure out if it's worth pursuing.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

Let me think through this carefully before responding.

The task: A user has a vague SaaS idea (freelancer invoice tracking) and wants to flesh it out and validate it.

The skill: bake-to-completion — explicitly for "half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea" that the user wants to "flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen." The skill says to run an "adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message."

This is a clear match. The user has explicitly said "it's still vague" and wants to "flesh it out and figure out if it's worth pursuing" — the exact use case the skill describes.

The skill mandates: ONE question per message (Socratic interview style), adaptive. So I should start with one concrete opening question, not dump all 8 dimensions at once.

Let me think about what the best opening question is. The space (freelancer invoice tracking) is extremely crowded — Wave, FreshBooks, HoneyBook, AND, Bonsai, Invoice Ninja, Zoho Invoice, QuickBooks, etc. The most critical unknown is: what differentiated insight does this person have that justifies building yet another one? The first question should probe the "why this, why you, why now" — specifically what pain they've observed that existing tools fail to solve.

Adversarial check on my own answer: Am I about to ask ONE question? Yes. Is it the right first question? The skill says "adaptive Socratic" — the opening should surface the core insight or lack thereof. The most load-bearing unknown is the problem insight / differentiation, not the target persona (which is stated: freelancers). Starting with "what's broken about existing tools for you or people you know?" gets at both the problem depth and whether this is personal-itch or observed pain.

```json
{
  "caseId": "sf-vague-saas-pitch",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Great — freelancer invoice tracking is a space worth interrogating carefully, because it's genuinely crowded (FreshBooks, Wave, Bonsai, HoneyBook, Invoice Ninja, and a dozen others all live here). That crowding isn't a reason to stop, but it does mean the bar for a differentiating insight is high.\n\nLet's work through this together with a Socratic interview. I'll ask one focused question at a time so we can surface what's actually interesting about your idea before evaluating it.\n\nOpening question:\n\nWhat specific friction have YOU (or people you know well) experienced with invoicing as a freelancer — and which existing tools did you try first, and where exactly did they fall short?",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Confirmed the request is a half-baked software/product idea seeking validation — clear skill match",
      "Identified the domain as software/SaaS — formal 8-dimension process applies",
      "Began adaptive Socratic interview with ONE opening question per skill mandate",
      "Chose opening question to surface problem insight and differentiation (most load-bearing unknown given crowded market)",
      "Did not dump scorecard or multiple questions — waited for user response first"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user is at the ideation stage, not already mid-build",
      "They have not yet run this through any prior validation process",
      "The conversation will continue with follow-up turns; this is message 1 of N"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Cannot complete the full 8-dimension scorecard or write the idea brief in a single turn — the skill requires an interview across multiple turns; this response is the correct first step only",
      "No file was written to ./idea-briefs/ yet — that happens after the interview concludes"
    ]
  }
}
```
